creator: bf1783@googlemail.com
text:
On 2/14/10, Rob Farmer <rfarmer@predatorlabs.net> wrote:
> I've updated the patch to include an OPTIONS dialog for some of the
> extra dependencies. I also fixed a couple of minor issues that I came
> across. The options I have are:
>
> OPTIONS=        ATLAS "Use Atlas instead of Blas" OFF \
>                 FFTW "Use FFTW" OFF \
>                 GUI "Build GUI (requires Java)" ON \
>                 HDF5 "Use HDF5 (requires GUI)" OFF \
>                 NLS "Build localization" OFF \
>                 MATIO "Matlab compatibility" OFF \
>                 PVM "Parallel Virtual Machine support" OFF \
>                 TK "TCL/TK support" OFF
>
> I'm not so sure about the what to make the defaults - any opinions?
>
> --
> Rob Farmer
>


I think that there ought to be an option to build without the gui, for
those people who only need the CLI, and want to avoid the java
dependencies.  I did this when I first made a port for 5.0, and the
java dependencies weren't in the tree.

What happens if fftw isn't used?  Is their some built-in fft code,
and, if so, how does it compare in speed and generality to fftw?  If
there isn't, or the built-in code is markedly slower or less flexible
than fftw, then this ought to be enabled by default, as the fft is
important in a lot of applications, and is not very costly in terms of
added disk space and build time.

Is the tcl-tk option still relevant, now that the gui is mostly
java-based? Are there still some wrappers to install?

Will any dependent ports (toolboxes) need any of the components that
are now disabled by default? If so, then we need to enable them by
default, or go through the hassle of defining PKGSUFFIXes and slave
ports.

b.
